summary: Alfred has a well-structured error handling foundation: a unified AlfredError type in alfred-core, a LispError type in alfred-lisp, and consistent use of Result<T> at domain boundaries. IO errors (file read/write) are propagated cleanly via thiserror. The main weaknesses are an unbounded undo stack (no size cap), hook callback errors that are silently stored in a side-channel buffer rather than propagated, and several unwrap() calls inside production-path code (not just tests) that could panic on unexpected terminal states. No empty catch blocks or swallowed exceptions were found in Rust terms -- errors are generally surfaced to the user via the editor message line.

swallowed_exceptions.context: let _ = runtime.eval("(dispatch-hook \"cursor-moved\")"); -- hook dispatch result is discarded via let _
let _ = runtime.eval("(dispatch-hook \"buffer-changed\")"); -- hook dispatch result is discarded
let _ = runtime.eval("(dispatch-hook \"mode-changed\")"); -- hook dispatch result is discarded
Err(_) => return (vec![], vec![]); -- I/O error on missing plugins directory is silently ignored
let _ = alfred_core::command::execute(state, cmd_name); -- command execution during macro replay, error discarded

missing_error_handling.description: The undo_stack has no size cap. Editing a large file for an extended session will cause unbounded memory growth. Unlike the change_list (capped at 100 entries) and jump_list (capped at JUMP_LIST_MAX_SIZE), the undo_stack grows without limit.
In test setup code, runtime.eval(...).unwrap_or_else(|e| panic!(...)) is used. While this is test-only code, it indicates a pattern of panic-on-error in setup helpers.
compute_edit_notification(...).unwrap() is called in test code -- if the function returns None in a test this will panic rather than fail gracefully.

locations: crates/alfred-tui/src/app.rs:400 -- ct_event::poll(Duration::from_millis(100)) prevents blocking forever on terminal events
crates/alfred-tui/src/app.rs:344 -- crossterm::terminal::size().unwrap_or((80, 24)) falls back to 80x24 on terminal size failure
crates/alfred-bin/src/main.rs:83 -- plugin load errors are collected and shown as a message rather than crashing
crates/alfred-lisp/src/bridge.rs:2380 -- list-dir returns empty list on any IO error rather than propagating

layer_analysis.pattern: Result<T, AlfredError> via thiserror -- clean and consistent
LispError wrapping ParseError and RuntimeError -- clean boundary
Errors returned as RuntimeError to Lisp, surfaced as editor messages -- reasonable
io::Result<()> at top level; deferred action errors shown as messages. Hook dispatch errors silently discarded via let _
Errors collected into Vec<String> and returned to caller -- reasonable for batch operations

cross_boundary_leaks.description: Hook errors are stored in a shared HookErrorBuffer (Rc<RefCell<Vec<String>>>) rather than propagated up. They are only surfaced if the dispatch-hook call checks the buffer after callbacks run. The cursor-moved/buffer-changed/mode-changed dispatch calls (app.rs:594-610) use let _ to discard the return value entirely, meaning hook errors from those dispatches are silently lost.

logging_assessment.notes: Alfred uses editor message line (state.message) as the primary error display mechanism rather than a logging framework. This is appropriate for a TUI application. There is no structured logging (log crate, tracing crate) -- errors visible to the user are in the message bar. Fatal startup errors use eprintln! (main.rs:28). Performance test output uses println! in test-only code. No sensitive data was found in error messages.

recommendations.title: Cap the undo_stack size to prevent unbounded memory growth
recommendations.description: The undo_stack in EditorState grows without limit. Each snapshot clones the entire ropey::Rope (O(1) due to structural sharing, but still allocates a new Rope handle and metadata). For very long editing sessions this will accumulate. Add a MAX_UNDO_DEPTH constant (e.g., 1000) matching the pattern already used for change_list (CHANGE_LIST_MAX_SIZE=100) and jump_list.

recommendations.title: Surface hook dispatch errors from cursor-moved, buffer-changed, mode-changed
recommendations.description: The three primary event hook dispatches in the event loop (app.rs:594-610) use 'let _ =' to discard the Result. These should at minimum be pattern-matched to detect LispError variants and display them as editor messages, consistent with how eval_and_display handles errors.

recommendations.title: Handle macro replay command errors explicitly
recommendations.description: During macro replay in input.rs:593, 'let _ = alfred_core::command::execute(state, cmd_name)' silently discards command errors. If a command in a macro fails, the macro continues silently. Consider breaking the replay loop on error and showing the error as a message.

recommendations.title: Add structured logging for diagnostic purposes
recommendations.description: The codebase has no logging framework (tracing/log). For debugging plugin load failures and hook errors in production, add optional structured logging (e.g., tracing with an env-filter) directed to a log file rather than stdout, to avoid interfering with the TUI.
